title: hebe masoniae
intro: Hebe masoniae, also known as Mason's hebe, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the hebe family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis and digestive disorders. Hebe masoniae is used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, and is also used in traditional Māori medicine in New Zealand, where it is valued for its potential health benefits.
